perturbation.original_text: 1.13 "Control" means the power, right or authority to direct or cause the direction of the management or policies of a Person, or to elect a majority of the board of directors or similar governing body of a Person, whether through the ownership of securities or similar ownership interest, by contract or otherwise, and references to "change of control" include the transfer, disposition or relinquishment, whether directly or indirectly, of Control.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.13 "Control" means the power, right or authority to direct or cause the direction of the management or policies of a Person, limited solely to operational matters, and excluding any influence over financial decisions or strategic planning. References to "change of control" do not include changes in operational management.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Control" is broad, encompassing the power to direct management and policies, including financial and strategic aspects. The modified definition restricts "Control" to operational matters only, explicitly excluding financial and strategic decisions. This contradicts Section 4.7 Actions Requiring Unanimous Board Approval, specifically 4.7.1 revising the budget or Business Plan of the Company; 4.7.5 consummating a Change of Control Transaction including without limitation merger, demerger, share exchange, liquidating or dissolving the Company, the entering into of a composition with creditors or the authorization of any filing for bankruptcy by the Company or the transformation of the Company into another type of legal entity; 4.7.6 entering into any agreement to effect a Change of Control Transaction or undertaking any action which effects a Change of Control Transaction, except pursuant to the exercise of the Put Option or Call Option, calls pursuant to Sections 7.5 and 7.6 and transfers permitted pursuant to Sections 8.1 and 8.2.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Article IV Management of the Company - 4.7
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Control' broadly encompasses directing management and policies, including financial and strategic aspects. The changed definition restricts 'Control' to operational matters, explicitly excluding financial and strategic decisions. This contradicts Section 4.7, particularly clauses 4.7.1 (revising the budget), 4.7.5 (Change of Control Transaction), and 4.7.6 (agreement to effect a Change of Control Transaction). These actions require unanimous board approval, implying control beyond purely operational matters, which now contradicts the restricted definition of 'Control'.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3.1 Conduct of Business. The Company shall conduct its Business in conformity with a business plan jointly prepared and agreed to by the Parties on or prior to the Effective Date, as may be amended from time to time after the Effective Date with the unanimous approval of the Board (the "Business Plan"). In case where the Parties have not agreed on the Business Plan as of the date hereof, then the Parties shall continue to discuss in good faith immediately following the execution of this Agreement and agree on the Business Plan prior to the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3.1 Conduct of Business. The Company may conduct its Business without regard to any prior business plan or budget. The Company shall have sole discretion over its business practices, without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates adherence to a mutually agreed-upon business plan, requiring unanimous board approval for amendments. The modified text grants the company unrestricted discretion over its business practices, explicitly removing any requirement to adhere to a prior business plan or budget. This change directly contradicts Sections 4.7 and 4.7.1 of Article IV, which requires unanimous board approval for revising the budget or Business Plan of the Company; making the enforcement of 4.7 and 4.7.1 uncertain.
perturbation.location: Article III Operation of the Company - 3.3.1
perturbation.contradicted_location: Article IV Management of the Company - 4.7
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 3.3.1 gives the Company unrestricted discretion over its business practices, removing the requirement to adhere to a mutually agreed-upon business plan and budget. This directly contradicts Article IV, Sections 4.7 and 4.7.1, which require unanimous board approval for revising the budget or Business Plan of the Company. The modified text makes the enforcement of Sections 4.7 and 4.7.1 uncertain, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the Company could act without board approval, despite 4.7 and 4.7.1 specifically requiring it.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1 AFSL's Right to Require the Purchase of the Shares by TPH or TPH-A. 9.1.1 As longs as AFSL and FSL are not in material and continuing breach of this Agreement or the Ancillary Agreements, FSL or AFSL may trigger the option to sell the Put Shares to TPH-A or its designee (the "Put Option") by providing a written notice of the exercise of the Put Option (the "Put Exercise Notice") simultaneously to the Company, TPH and TPH-A, which notice states (i) such Party's bona fide intention to exercise the Put Option, and (ii) the date on which such Party intends that the Put Closing Date occur.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 AFSL's Right to Require the Purchase of the Shares by TPH or TPH-A. 9.1.1 Irrespective of whether AFSL and FSL are in material and continuing breach of this Agreement or the Ancillary Agreements, FSL or AFSL may trigger the option to sell the Put Shares to TPH-A or its designee (the "Put Option") by providing a written notice of the exercise of the Put Option (the "Put Exercise Notice") simultaneously to the Company, TPH and TPH-A, which notice states (i) such Party's bona fide intention to exercise the Put Option, and (ii) the date on which such Party intends that the Put Closing Date occur.
perturbation.explanation: Original text states AFSL can only exercise the Put Option, if they are not in material breach of contract. Changing the start of the sentance to "Irrespective of whether AFSL and FSL are in material and continuing breach of this Agreement or the Ancillary Agreements" creates an In-Text Contradiction, due to a party exercising rights, even though they are in material breach.
perturbation.location: Article IX Put Option and Call Option - 9.1
perturbation.contradicted_location: Article IX Put Option and Call Option - 9.1
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 AFSL's Right to Require the Purchase of the Shares by TPH or TPH-A. 9.1.1 As longs as AFSL and FSL are not in material and continuing breach of this Agreement or the Ancillary Agreements, FSL or AFSL may trigger the option to sell the Put Shares to TPH-A or its designee (the "Put Option") by providing a written notice of the exercise of the Put Option (the "Put Exercise Notice") simultaneously to the Company, TPH and TPH-A, which notice states (i) such Party's bona fide intention to exercise the Put Option, and (ii) the date on which such Party intends that the Put Closing Date occur.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that AFSL can only trigger the Put Option if they are not in material breach. The changed text explicitly allows AFSL to trigger the Put Option irrespective of whether they are in material breach, which directly contradicts the condition stated in both the original text and the contradicted text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, as the changed clause directly conflicts with the original condition for exercising the Put Option.
